reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement precisely. G₁ and G₂ are arbitrary groups with G = G₁ × G₂ encoded as the product type. H is an arbitrary subgroup of G₁ × G₂, and H.Normal captures H ⊴ G. The intersection hypotheses use Subgroup.prod ⊤ ⊥ (= G₁ × {1}, the canonical image of G₁ in G) and Subgroup.prod ⊥ ⊤ (= {1} × G₂, the canonical image of G₂ in G), which is the standard mathematical interpretation of "H ∩ G_i = {1}". The conclusion H ≤ Subgroup.center (G₁ × G₂) exactly encodes H ≤ Z(G). No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure (universal over G₁, G₂, H) is faithfully preserved.
